title: hieracium rohacsense subsp. poecilostictum
intro: Hieracium rohacsense subsp. poecilostictum, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is said to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Hieracium rohacsense subsp. poecilostictum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is believed to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and health benefits of this plant.
